root_cause: Cluster autoscaler cannot add nodes because cloud provider quota is exhausted
evidence: Scheduler reported insufficient capacity: 0/10 nodes are available: 10 Insufficient cpu, 10 Insufficient memory.
Cluster autoscaler reported ScaleUpFailed: failed to increase node group size: googleapi: Error 403: QUOTA_EXCEEDED: Quota 'CPUS_ALL_REGIONS' exceeded. Limit: 240.0 globally.
Cluster autoscaler status reports cloud quota exhaustion
No successful Scheduled event observed after quota failure
Scheduler/autoscaler quota failure window lasted 7.0 minutes

causes.message: Scheduler could not place the Pod on existing nodes because cluster capacity was insufficient
Cluster autoscaler could not create replacement nodes because cloud provider quota was exhausted
Pod remains Pending until cloud quota is increased, released, or capacity is added elsewhere

pod:ml-batch-trainer-85f6d7c985-p9sz8: Pod is Pending after scheduler capacity rejection and cloud quota exhaustion
Scheduler message: 0/10 nodes are available: 10 Insufficient cpu, 10 Insufficient memory.
controller:cluster-autoscaler: ScaleUpFailed: failed to increase node group size: googleapi: Error 403: QUOTA_EXCEEDED: Quota 'CPUS_ALL_REGIONS' exceeded. Limit: 240.0 globally.

Cluster-autoscaler status at 2026-05-16 11:27:00 +0000 UTC: ScaleUp: Backoff (GCE quota exceeded: QUOTA_EXCEEDED: Quota 'CPUS_ALL_REGIONS' exceeded. Limit: 240.0 globally); NodeGroups: prod-gpu-pool currentSize=10 targetSize=10
configmap:cluster-autoscaler-status: Cluster-autoscaler status at 2026-05-16 11:27:00 +0000 UTC: ScaleUp: Backoff (GCE quota exceeded: QUOTA_EXCEEDED: Quota 'CPUS_ALL_REGIONS' exceeded. Limit: 240.0 globally); NodeGroups: prod-gpu-pool currentSize=10 targetSize=10

likely_causes: AWS vCPU, instance, ENI, or address limits prevent node creation
GCP regional CPU, address, disk, or project quota prevents node creation
Azure VM family cores, public IP, or regional quota prevents node creation
Cluster autoscaler found a fitting node group but the cloud provider rejected scale-up for quota reasons
suggested_checks: kubectl -n kube-system get configmap cluster-autoscaler-status -o yaml
Check cloud provider quota dashboards for regional CPU, VM, IP, disk, or instance limits
Request a quota increase or scale a node group in a region/zone with available quota
